frame_analyses.description: Initial view of laparoscopic trainer with Penrose drain positioned on white gauze
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Standard EMIG setup with Penrose drain visible, no visible marks for targeting

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver entering field from left, suture visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper instrument introduction, needle appears to be loaded on needle driver

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver positioned near Penrose drain, preparing for first pass
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good positioning for suture placement through the slit

frame_analyses.description: Suture being placed through Penrose drain with both instruments working
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated bimanual technique with needle driver and grasper

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot construction with suture ends positioned for first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Appears to be setting up for surgeon's knot construction

frame_analyses.description: First throw being tightened with instruments manipulating suture
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good instrument control during knot tightening phase
Continuing with standard intracorporeal knot tying technique

frame_analyses.description: Final view showing completed knot with suture tails visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot appears secure with good approximation of drain edges

knot_assessments.notes: First throw appears to be double throw (surgeon's knot) based on EMIG protocol
Second throw single with appropriate hand alternation
Third throw completing the knot with hand alternation

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Good approximation of drain edges with secure knot, no visible gaps

technique_summary: This appears to be an EMIG tutorial demonstration showing proper intracorporeal knot tying technique on a Penrose drain. The technique demonstrates good instrument control and appropriate knot construction.
improvement_suggestions: This appears to be a demonstration video rather than a timed performance
Focus on maintaining this level of precision in actual timed scenarios
strengths: Proper surgeon's knot construction on first throw
Good tissue approximation and knot security
Appropriate instrument handling and bimanual coordination
